summary: Verified Stats counter regression fix on clouDroplets.IO home page. Scrolled to stats-section (dark bg) on desktop 1440x900; all four counters animated and settled correctly to the expected values on both first load and after reload.
critical_code_review_comments: Stats.jsx: section-level useInView + rAF Counter with explicit setN(value) at completion is correct and reliably renders 250+ for first counter.

Only console warning observed is framer-motion advising a non-static position on a container; harmless but could be addressed by adding position:relative to the parent using useInView/whileInView if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stats fix verified. Values read from [data-testid='stats-section'] .tabular-nums = ['250+','120+','16','40+'] across two runs (initial + reload). No console errors. Only a benign framer-motion warning about container position.
rca of the issue: Previous regressions used per-counter useInView which could miss the first counter due to Lenis smooth scroll + margin/threshold timing. Current fix uses a single section-level useInView(once:true, margin:'-10%') passing `start` prop to each Counter, and Counter forces setN(value) on completion, guaranteeing final value even if rAF is throttled/cancelled.
